rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by ensuring property associations reference valid identifiers in scope.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when needed.
generic_rules: Only reference visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE clauses are present in the current scope.
Avoid generating property associations that point to undefined or inaccessible elements.
Ensure AGREE guarantees and assumptions align with the scoping rules of component types vs. implementations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must reside within `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees over visible ports; implementation annexes handle subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not add property associations that reference identifiers not declared or visible in the current scope.
Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes without proper relocation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about Byte_Count property associations
